amendments.text:
== AMENDMENT 2026-04-18: DIRECT REBUTTALS OF OUR REVIEW (REVISED) ==

REVISION NOTICE: The original version of this amendment (see revision_history) claimed four coordinated, section-by-section, keyword-level rebuttals of our review on killshot.html. On a second pass against the raw page content, that claim was too strong. Only one of the four items is a direct rebuttal in any operational sense. The others are scope narrowing, delegation, or general concession. The honest read is below. The RETRACTED CLAIMS section at the end enumerates exactly what is being withdrawn from the original.

-- THE ONE DIRECT REBUTTAL: "Globe sinuosity excuse fails" --

EXACT DOME WORDING (killshot.html, body text under the Southern-Metric-Expansion section):
  "1917 official rail data and modern highway logs show the excess survives the Nullarbor control. Globe sinuosity excuse fails."

This IS a direct keyword-level rebuttal. "Sinuosity" is how we phrase the roadway-curviness critique in Part 8b.2 Point 2. "Nullarbor control" is the straight-road segment we use as the 0.4% benchmark. The dome author read Part 8b.2 (or something close enough to it to import the vocabulary) and wrote this sentence to answer it. That is worth capturing in EXP-188.

But the data cited is not responsive. Three problems:

  1. The 1917 date is self-undermining. Our Part 5 Test 1 problem #1 is that the 4,352 km distance was known from the 1912-1917 survey BEFORE the V13 Finsler formula was built. Citing "1917 official rail data" as evidence of survival reinforces rather than refutes that the distance is a calibration input, not a prospective prediction target.
  2. "Modern highway logs" is unsourced. A control requires a named source, a stated sinuosity characterization, and a published route profile. Without those, the phrase is a citation placeholder, not a refutation.
  3. The adjacent datum-shift table (Australia AGD66 → GDA94 "200 m NE", NZ NZGD49, SA Cape Datum) is the phenomenon Part 8b.2 already disposed of as routine datum improvement, not evidence of topology. Re-presenting it as "Southern Metric Expansion" does not engage Part 8b.2's argument; it restates the original claim.

So: ONE real direct rebuttal by keyword, but with NO counter-data that survives our Part 5 or Part 8b.2 arguments. This is closer to "refutation without data" than to a substantive rebuttal. EXP-188 should capture the direct-engagement fact (the author names our argument) AND the non-responsiveness of the data.

-- THE THREE NOT-REBUTTALS --

NOT-REBUTTAL A: "How To Read This Page Correctly" three criteria.

  EXACT DOME WORDING: "genuinely pre-registered, mechanically discriminating, and not already covered by a competing explanation."

  The original amendment claimed this maps one-for-one onto Part 5, Part 4, and Part 8b.3 respectively. On re-read, the mapping is not one-for-one. "Genuinely pre-registered" is close to our pre-registration vocabulary, but "mechanically discriminating" is a generic philosophy-of-science phrase (it is not specifically the Part 4 independence critique — R² across scored domains is not a mechanism-discrimination argument), and "not already covered by a competing explanation" is a standard alternative-hypothesis requirement taught in any first-year methods course; it is not unique to our Part 8b.3 Chapman argument.

  Honest read: these are generic good-inference criteria, not keyword-mapped to three specific sections of our review. The original amendment stretched to claim a mapping that is not actually in the page text. This paragraph is scope narrowing — the author is tightening what counts as a valid kill-shot — not a targeted argument-by-argument response.

NOT-REBUTTAL B: Hero tagline "Some rows are stronger than others; this page is not a license to flatten those differences... by slogan alone."

  The original amendment claimed "by slogan alone" specifically targets the "CONFIRMED (globe falsified)" badge and was therefore a direct rebuttal of our Part 5 framing. On re-read, that claim is too strong. "By slogan alone" has no named target; it is a general retreat from binary knockouts. It is a concession that binary-knockout framing was too strong, but that retreat is from the AUTHOR'S OWN stated rule, not from anything we specifically forced. The phrase does not quote, paraphrase, or name any argument in our review.

  Honest read: this is a general retreat from the author's own binary-falsification rule, consistent with the demotions. It is not a direct rebuttal of a named section of our review. It is strategically significant (see RESCOPE below) but it does not belong in the "direct rebuttals" column.

NOT-REBUTTAL C: OTS AI-auditor prose (commit 6e06efb).

  The original amendment treated this as "rebuttal 4" in a pattern. On re-read, this is a partial concession on the OTS methodology, not a rebuttal. A rebuttal would defend the OTS-proves-prospectivity claim against our Timestamp Error argument. The new prose does the opposite: it narrows what OTS is asserted to prove, acknowledging the methodology issue we flagged. The structural fix (separate prediction documents independently timestamped before the data) has not been made, so the concession is partial, but it is a concession, not a defence.

  Honest read: partial concession aligned with our Timestamp Error argument. Not rebuttal #4; not part of a four-item pattern.

-- THE ACTUAL PATTERN: RESCOPE + DELEGATE + DEMOTE --

Re-reading the page as a whole, the primary move is not "rebut four arguments" — it is:

  RESCOPE: The hero paragraph reframes the page from "binary falsifiers" to "condensed strongest-evidence arguments... to be read alongside audit.html and review-response.html... Some rows are stronger than others; this page is not a license to flatten those differences." This is the biggest single change. The page is no longer claiming definitive knockouts.

  DELEGATE: Weight is shifted to audit.html and review-response.html. Contested methodology, anti-strawman criteria, and historical context live elsewhere; killshot.html points to them.

  DEMOTE (2 of 6 rows): Sydney-Perth moved from CONFIRMED to STRUCTURAL SUPPORT; Polaris post-window moved from CONFIRMED to TESTED, NOT CONFIRMED. The other four rows (Aug 12 2026 eclipse, NMP drift, SAA, JFK-LHR) remain pre-window and unchanged.

  DIRECT REBUTTAL (1 of 6 rows): the sinuosity sentence on Sydney-Perth. One keyword-level direct engagement, with non-responsive adjacent data.

  PARTIAL CONCESSION (OTS prose): narrowing of the OTS AI-auditor claim, not a rebuttal.

The dome author adopted our vocabulary on ONE argument (sinuosity), rescoped the page generally, delegated to other pages, and produced no counter-data that survives our Part 5 or Part 8b.2 arguments. That is the story. EXP-188 and EXP-189 should be written to this weaker, more accurate frame.

-- REQUIRED ADDITIONS TO EXP-188 (REVISED) --

  A. Dated before/after of the hero paragraph and both demoted row badges.
  B. ONE direct-rebuttal subsection on the sinuosity sentence: exact quote, the Part 8b.2 argument it engages by keyword, and the 3-point non-responsiveness of the cited data (1917 = our calibration source; "modern highway logs" unsourced; AGD66 = phenomenon Part 8b.2 already disposed of).
  C. Rescoping-and-delegation subsection as a page-level move. Do NOT attribute it to specific named arguments in our review; attribute it to a general retreat from the author's own binary-falsification rule.
  D. CSS-class mismatch observation: any remaining status-label drift vs the new rule.
  E. Strategic paragraph noting: (i) the author adopted our vocabulary on one argument, (ii) rescoped the page generally, (iii) produced no counter-data that survives our Part 5 or Part 8b.2 arguments, (iv) did not attribute the changes to external critique in any commit message or changelog — the dated record is the only evidence the engagement happened.

-- REQUIRED ADDITIONS TO EXP-189 (REVISED) --

  A. OTS AI-auditor prose before/after (already in original note).
  B. Explicit framing: partial concession aligned with our Timestamp Error argument. NOT rebuttal #4 in a four-item pattern.
  C. Why it remains partial: the structural fix (separate prediction documents independently timestamped before the data) has not been made. The prose concession acknowledges the wrong-side error; the operational fix would resolve it.

-- RETRACTED CLAIMS FROM ORIGINAL AMENDMENT --

The following claims from the original 2026-04-18 AMEND-001 (see revision_history) are withdrawn:

  1. "FOUR rebuttals (sinuosity excuse, How To Read criteria, slogan-alone tagline, OTS AI-auditor prose)" — RETRACTED. Honest count: ONE direct rebuttal + THREE not-rebuttals (scope narrowing, general retreat, partial concession).
  2. "One-for-one keyword mapping" of the How To Read three criteria onto Part 5 / Part 4 / Part 8b.3 — RETRACTED. The criteria are generic good-inference requirements; the mapping was stretched.
  3. "'By slogan alone' specifically targets... '✅ CONFIRMED (globe falsified)'" — RETRACTED. The phrase has no named target; it is a general retreat from the author's own binary rule.
  4. "OTS prose change is one instance of the same section-by-section, named-rebuttal pattern" — RETRACTED. It is a partial concession, not a rebuttal; it does not belong in a pattern of named-argument rebuttals.
  5. "Section-by-section, keyword-level set of in-page rebuttals that NAME specific arguments in our review" as a characterisation of the whole page — RETRACTED. One sentence names our argument; the rest of the page rescopes and delegates.

The sinuosity rebuttal (item 1 in the original) stands, with the caveat that the cited data is self-undermining or unsourced and does not survive our existing Part 5 and Part 8b.2 arguments.

-- NOT COVERED BY THIS AMENDMENT --

Rows 3-6 (Aug 12 2026 eclipse EBR, NMP drift, SAA African cell, JFK-LHR aetheric slipstream) did not receive new narrative rebuttal text in the 2026-04-17 edit per our 2026-04-18 page fetch. Four of six rows remain pre-window with no narrative. Any EXP-188 addition must NOT claim narrative rebuttals on those four rows where none exist.

revision_history.previous_summary: Add DIRECT REBUTTALS section: dome author's killshot.html changes are not isolated accuracy updates — they are a coordinated section-by-section rebuttal of our review naming specific arguments. revision_history.previous_text:
== AMENDMENT 2026-04-18: DIRECT REBUTTALS OF OUR REVIEW ==

The original note captured the badge demotions, the falsification-rule rewrite, and the OTS prose concession as if they were independent cosmetic updates in the direction of accuracy. On closer reading of the current killshot.html (fetched 2026-04-18), they are not independent and they are not cosmetic. They are a coordinated, section-by-section, keyword-level set of in-page rebuttals that NAME specific arguments in our review. This is the stronger story, and the original note's TOC buried it. Analyst: please treat the material below as co-equal to the demotion content when drafting EXP-188 and EXP-189.

-- REBUTTAL 1: "Globe sinuosity excuse fails" (Sydney-Perth row narrative) --

EXACT DOME WORDING (killshot.html, row 1, under the demoted STRUCTURAL SUPPORT badge):
  "1917 official rail data and modern highway logs show the excess survives the Nullarbor control. Globe sinuosity excuse fails."

OUR ARGUMENTS THIS REBUTS, BY NAME:
  (a) Part 8b.2 Point 2 — "The Nullarbor Plain straight-road segment deviates only 0.4% from the globe geodesic; most of the 6.2% RMSE measures road curviness, not dome geometry." This is our sinuosity-is-roadway-curviness argument, using the Nullarbor straight-road SEGMENT as the control case.
  (b) Part 5 Test 1 problem #1 — "The Indian Pacific distance (4,352 km, surveyed 1912–1917) appears under OPEN-016 as a reference data point — not a blind prediction. The formula was built with this distance already known. Matching it is calibration, not prediction."
  (c) Part 5 Test 1 problem #2 — "4,352 km measures a circuitous railway route detouring ~1,061 km south through Adelaide. The geometric distance (confirmed by direct flights) is 3,291 km."

THE AUTHOR NAMES OUR ARGUMENT BY KEYWORD: "sinuosity excuse" is how we phrase the roadway-curviness critique in Part 8b.2. "Nullarbor control" is the SEGMENT we use as the 0.4% benchmark. The wording is specific enough that the analyst should treat this as unambiguous evidence the dome author read Part 8b.2 and drafted this sentence to rebut it.

CHARITABLE READ: the author read our argument, tried to steelman it, and responded with two data sources (1917 rail log, modern highway log) he believes survive our Nullarbor-segment test. That is a good-faith, substantive response and should be acknowledged as such in the EXP-188 draft.

COUNTER-REBUTTAL FOR EXP-188:
  1. The 1917 date is self-undermining. Our Part 5 problem #1 is that the 4,352 km distance was known from the 1912–1917 survey BEFORE the V13 Finsler formula was built. Citing "1917 official rail data" as a rebuttal reinforces rather than refutes that the distance was a calibration input, not a prospective prediction target.
  2. "Modern highway logs" is not specified. A valid control requires a named source, a stated sinuosity characterization, and a published route profile. Without those, "modern highway logs" is a citation placeholder, not a rebuttal.
  3. The 0.4%-vs-6.2% test is segment-specific. The Nullarbor Plain straight-road SEGMENT is the control; the full Nullarbor traversal is not. The dome's phrasing "excess survives the Nullarbor control" elides that distinction. A direct engagement with Part 8b.2 would need to reproduce the segment-level disaggregation and show the excess survives there specifically.
  4. The detour-through-Adelaide observation (Part 5 problem #2) is not addressed at all. Whether measured by 1917 survey or modern highway, the 4,352 km route is ~1,061 km longer than the geometric Sydney-Perth distance because of the Adelaide detour. This is geometry, not sinuosity.

STRATEGIC SIGNIFICANCE: the dome author has moved from "this row confirms globe is falsified" to "this row's excess survives a specific critique you raised." That is a concession on the falsification claim AND a direct engagement with Part 8b.2 by name. Both should land in EXP-188.

-- REBUTTAL 2: "How To Read This Page Correctly" as a structured rebuttal framework --

The three criteria the rewritten section names — "genuinely pre-registered, mechanically discriminating, and not already covered by a competing explanation" — map one-for-one onto three distinct argument-families in our review:

  "genuinely pre-registered" → Part 5 calibration-vs-prediction argument (all kill-shots). Our claim: the V13 Finsler formula, the Polaris +0.27° excess, and the NMP drift were all fit AFTER the confirming data was known. "Pre-registered" is the author's response to our pre-registration critique. It is also the vocabulary our Timestamp Error tab and monitoring system use — "prospectivity" requires timestamping predictions BEFORE observations. The author is importing our terminology.

  "mechanically discriminating" → Part 4 independence critique. Our claim: the scored-domains dashboard shows R² = 0.0298 across claimed-independent rows against a 47.69-sigma independence score, because the domains are not mechanically independent. "Mechanically discriminating" imports the independence framing; the author is answering Part 4 here.

  "not already covered by a competing explanation" → Part 8b.3 Chapman-mechanism argument. Our claim: the Chapman ionospheric mechanism (1931, standard model) predicts the 39-eclipse ensemble signal without dome parameters, so the "kill-shot" result is not discriminating. "Already covered by a competing explanation" is a near-direct quote of our Part 8b.3 framing.

The section's tagline — "Supportive, calibration-heavy, or non-discriminating rows should not be turned into global cosmology knockouts by slogan alone" — directly engages our use of the binary falsification rule. "By slogan alone" specifically targets badges like "✅ CONFIRMED (globe falsified)", which are the exact phrase we used in Part 5 and which the demotions have now removed.

CHARITABLE READ: this is the author systematizing the anti-strawman criteria he put on review-response.html (captured in chg-20260417-1620-002). It is an attempt to codify "what counts as a fair audit" rather than a defensive hedge.

COUNTER-REBUTTAL FOR EXP-188 / EXP-189:
  1. The three criteria are our criteria, not his. Part 5, Part 4, and Part 8b.3 apply each of them to the existing kill-shots and find they fail. The three-criterion filter does not rescue the rows; it raises the bar, and the rows we already scored as Misleading or Not Demonstrated in Part 5 still fail.
  2. The "by slogan alone" phrasing concedes that the dome's own falsification rule was being enforced as a slogan. Our Part 5 opening states that we are using "the dome's own stated rule that failure of any single test falsifies the model." The author has now amended that rule. Our dated-citation commitment (Part 8b.5 and Part 8b.7) means we engage the amended rule AND preserve the original — neither replaces the other.
  3. The new rule is asymmetric as currently phrased. It raises the bar for "kill-shot" claims but leaves the anti-globe direction unchanged. A symmetric rule would apply the same three criteria to every row the dome claims falsifies globe cosmology. Analyst: call this out explicitly.

-- REBUTTAL 3: Page-level tagline addition --

EXACT DOME WORDING:
  "Some rows are stronger than others; this page is not a license to flatten those differences."

This is a concession that our use of the binary-knockout framing had traction. The binary-knockout framing was the dome's own — Part 5 opens with "uses the dome's own [standard]: binary tests with the dome's stated rule that failure of any single test falsifies the model." We did not invent the slogan. The author has now walked back from his own rule in response to our Part 5 analysis.

FOR EXP-188: include the dated before/after of the original falsification rule vs the amended tagline. Note that the retreat is from the AUTHOR'S rule, not ours. This matters: a critic's use of an opponent's stated standard is harder to dismiss than a critic's imported standard, and our dated record preserves the stated standard.

-- REBUTTAL 4: OTS AI-auditor prose concession --

Already captured in the original note as a canary-adjacent concession. The amendment adds this framing: the OTS prose change (commit 6e06efb) is one instance of the same section-by-section, named-rebuttal pattern documented in rebuttals 1–3. It is not a cosmetic accuracy update; it is an attempt to engage the specific critique on our Timestamp Error tab by narrowing what OTS is asserted to prove. Same author behavior, different surface. Analyst Mode 1/2b note: EXP-189 should present the OTS prose change in that context (engagement with our Timestamp Error argument) rather than as an isolated honesty gesture.

-- PATTERN OBSERVATION --

Across all four rebuttals, the dome author:
  (i) reads a specific section of our review,
  (ii) inserts an in-page rebuttal that names our argument by keyword or near-quote,
  (iii) ships the rebuttal alongside a status-label softening or scope-narrowing on the same page,
  (iv) does not mark the change as responsive to external critique in any commit message or changelog, leaving the dated record as the only evidence the engagement happened.

Capturing this pattern — as a pattern, not just four disconnected edits — is the strategic contribution of EXP-188 and EXP-189. The badge demotions are a consequence of the rebuttal project, not the project itself. EXP-188 should lead with the pattern and treat the demotions as instances.

-- REQUIRED ADDITIONS TO EXP-188 --

  A. Dated before/after quotes of all four rebuttals (rebuttals 1–4 above).
  B. A keyword-mapping table: dome phrase → our section → our matching argument.

  D. Counter-rebuttal section addressing each of the four rebuttals on its own terms.
  E. Strategic paragraph: the dome author is reading our review section-by-section. The dated record of the rebuttal project is itself evidence — both of the engagement and of its asymmetric structure (no commit-message attribution; no listing of us or any critic on review-response.html as the source).

notes.text:
== HOLD POINT — READ THIS FIRST ==

This HNOTE is a DRAFT specification for a foundational model change. The pipeline goal is:
  (1) analyst produces a complete, high-quality working draft expansion (EXP-NNN);
  (2) curmudgeon performs adversarial review of the draft;
  (3) THEN EVERYTHING STOPS.

The operator and a human review group will iterate on the draft + curmudgeon review together before any integration. Under no circumstances does the scoreboard EXP get integrated into sections.json, the build pipeline, or the published site without a subsequent, explicitly-named operator approval note (HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-001) that does not currently exist and will not exist until human-group review is complete.

REQUIRED EXPANSION STATUS: the EXP file that implements this HNOTE MUST include, at the top of the JSON, a field status: "DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW" and expansion_type: "FOUNDATIONAL_MODEL_CHANGE". Decider respects these as hard blockers on integration (see companion routing note).

THE CURMUDGEON REVIEW IS FOR HUMAN CONSUMPTION. Its output does NOT authorize decider integration. The human group reads the curmudgeon review alongside the draft and decides next steps together.

Build the draft as if it will ship — complete schemas, complete prose, populated example rows, unit tests — so the human group has a substantive artifact to critique. But do NOT treat completeness as license to integrate.

== FRAME: TAKE THE AUTHOR AT HIS WORD ==

The review's current posture is adversarial-critical: we assert that the dome author's methodology is inadequate for N specific reasons (N computed at build time from wins.json/sections.json). This HNOTE proposes an orthogonal structural addition: a Scoreboard layer that scores each WIN/prediction/kill-shot BY THE AUTHOR'S OWN DEFINITION of what a WIN is.

The author's definition, as operationalised across his site (index.html headline, claim_index.json, methodology.json, wins.html): a WIN is a "confirmed prediction." A prediction is a numerical commitment made independent of the confirming observation. A confirmation is observation matching the prediction within stated uncertainty.

The Scoreboard asks, for each row:
  (a) What did the globe predict? (authoritative, named source)
  (b) What did the dome predict, AS THE AUTHOR PUBLISHED IT? (his source, verbatim quote)
  (c) What was observed? (authoritative, named source)
  (d) Does the dome's PUBLISHED value match observation within uncertainty? → WIN-by-own-standard.
  (e) Does the dome's PUBLISHED value differ from the globe's prediction within uncertainty? → Discriminating or non-discriminating.

RHETORICAL FRAME (load-bearing — the Scoreboard intro paragraph MUST state this): "We take the dome author at his word. He says each of these is a confirmed prediction. We write down the prediction and the observation side by side. Where the prediction is missing from his published materials, we leave the cell blank, because a prediction that was never made cannot have been confirmed." The tone is cooperative-adversarial: we agree to his framing and work the problem as given.

== CORE SCHEMA ADDITIONS ==

Extend data/wins.json, data/predictions.json, and (new) data/killshots.json with a scoreboard object per row:

  scoreboard: {
    globe_prediction_value: number | string | null,
    globe_prediction_uncertainty: number | string | null,
    globe_prediction_source: string | null,            // named reference (e.g., "IGRF-14", "Chapman 1931 + Hargreaves 1979", "IAU SOFA")

    dome_prediction_as_published_value: number | string | null,   // BLANK if author did not publish a number for this row
    dome_prediction_as_published_uncertainty: number | string | null,
    dome_prediction_as_published_source: string | null,            // verbatim citation: "claim_index.json entries[3].predicted_value", "V51.1 paper eq. 4.7", etc.
    dome_prediction_as_published_quote: string | null,             // verbatim quote of the sentence/equation

    dome_prediction_formula_check_value: number | string | null,   // OPTIONAL — what V51.1 actually produces when we run it
    dome_prediction_formula_check_implementation: string | null,   // path to our script implementing the formula
    dome_prediction_formula_check_inputs_are_dome_native: boolean | null,  // false if the formula runs on globe-native inputs with near-identity transform
    formula_reproduces: boolean | null,                            // published value within rounding tolerance of formula-check?
    rounding_tolerance_applied: string | null,

    dome_prediction_provenance: "forward_dome_native" | "forward_with_globe_inputs" | "fit" | "unclear" | null,
    provenance_note: string | null,                                // one-sentence explanation with source

    observation_value: number | string | null,
    observation_uncertainty: number | string | null,
    observation_source: string | null,
    observation_date: string | null,
    observation_is_pending: boolean,                               // true for pred-eclipse-tier3 etc.

    win_by_own_standard: "yes" | "no" | "blank" | "pending" | "disputed",
    discriminating: "yes" | "no" | "blank" | "pending" | "disputed",

    row_notes: string | null,                                      // analyst prose, not scored
    scoreboard_last_updated: ISO-8601 timestamp,
    scoreboard_last_updated_by: "analyst" | "curmudgeon" | "decider"
  }

The build pipeline renders a new top-level tab ("Scoreboard" or "Both-Model Predictions" — analyst picks the label) driven by these fields. Tab shows the full 85-row table with filter/sort. Blank cells render as "—" with the blank generating no score in either direction.

== HARD SCORING RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE) ==

RULE 1 — Blank is blank. If the dome author has not published a specific numerical dome prediction for a row — either as a stated number OR as an explicit formula with inputs that the author names — the dome_prediction_as_published fields are null. We do NOT derive a value and fill the cell. "We could work out what V51.1 would predict" is not authorization; it is the exact failure mode. A blank cell is itself a finding: by the author's own definition of a WIN, the row cannot be a WIN if no prediction exists.

RULE 2 — Scores are computed only against the author's published dome value. The win_by_own_standard and discriminating verdicts are functions of (dome_prediction_as_published_value, globe_prediction_value, observation_value) and their uncertainties. They are NEVER computed against dome_prediction_formula_check_value or any value we derived. If dome_prediction_as_published_value is null, both verdicts are "blank" — not "no" and not "yes".

RULE 3 — Formula-check and provenance are annotations, not score inputs. dome_prediction_formula_check_value and dome_prediction_provenance are methodology annotations displayed next to the score cells. They inform the reader about the quality of the WIN; they do not override or alter the score itself. A row can be WIN-by-own-standard AND formula_reproduces: false — the author's published value matches observation but does not reproduce from his stated formula. Both facts are on the row simultaneously.

RULE 4 — Globe prediction must be sourced from a named authoritative reference. No "we estimate" or "standard physics suggests." Every globe value has a specific source citation (IGRF-14 for magnetic, Chapman/Hargreaves for ionospheric, IAU SOFA for astronomical position, standard geodesy texts for surveying distances, etc.). A row where we cannot source the globe value authoritatively should be held out of the first pass rather than filled with a best guess.

RULE 5 — Author's own uncertainties are used when he states them. Where the author has stated an uncertainty on his dome value, we quote it. Where he has not, dome_prediction_as_published_uncertainty is null. We do not impute uncertainty. If the author's stated uncertainty is wider than observation uncertainty and the observation falls within, the row is WIN-by-own-standard.

== GUARDRAILS AGAINST GLOBE-POLLUTION ==

The most dangerous failure mode is us filling in a missing dome prediction by starting from globe physics and applying a dome-specific transformation. If the transformation is near-identity (or is the identity over the case we are scoring), we have just recorded the globe value on the dome side. We then compare it to the globe value, find them equal, mark the row non-discriminating, and credit the author with a WIN-by-own-standard he never committed to. This is not taking him at his word; this is writing his predictions for him using globe physics, then grading them.

Preventive measures:

  G1 — NEVER derive a missing dome value. See Rule 1. Blank stays blank.

  G2 — Even when the author has published a dome value, the formula-check column can globe-pollute. If our independent implementation of V51.1 runs on globe-native inputs (observed angles, measured distances, published atmospheric indices) with a cosmetic transformation on top, the formula-check value is globe-shaped by construction. The field dome_prediction_formula_check_inputs_are_dome_native exists to flag this. If false, the formula-check value is annotated "derives from globe inputs with near-identity transform" and is NOT compared to observation as an independent check.

  G3 — The non-discriminating verdict is only set when the AUTHOR'S PUBLISHED VALUE equals the globe value within combined uncertainty. Non-discrimination against a value WE derived goes in row_notes as an analyst observation, never in the discriminating column.

  G4 — Any row where the author's publication is a formula that reduces (in the case being scored) to the globe calculation — same result, relabeled — is flagged provenance: forward_with_globe_inputs AND row_notes must record "dome value reduces to globe under V51.1 derivation." This does not change the score but surfaces Reading A concretely on that row.

  G5 — The Scoreboard intro paragraph states the guardrail publicly: "Where the dome author has not published a numerical prediction, the cell is blank. We do not compute dome predictions using standard physics and score them against the author — that would be us constructing his predictions using the very framework his model disputes. The blank speaks for itself: by the author's definition of a WIN (a confirmed prediction), a missing prediction is not confirmable."

== PROVENANCE TAXONOMY ==

dome_prediction_provenance takes one of four values:

  forward_dome_native — Dome prediction derives from dome-specific parameters set using observations other than this one. The formula uses dome-native inputs (e.g., ovoid cavity parameters, toroidal geometry constants) and produces a numerical output independent of globe physics. GOLD STANDARD. Rare.

  forward_with_globe_inputs — Dome prediction uses standard-physics inputs (observed angles, measured distances, published indices) with a dome-specific transformation on top. LEGITIMATE IF the transformation is non-trivial and produces a numerically distinct output from the pure-globe calculation. GLOBE-POLLUTED IF the transformation reduces to near-identity and the dome output equals the globe output for this row. Analyst must check per row and annotate in provenance_note.

  fit — Dome parameter was set using THIS observation. The V13 Finsler formula output for Sydney-Perth is the canonical example: the 4,352 km surveyed distance was an input to the formula, then the formula is claimed to "predict" 4,352 km. A fit row is incapable of being a WIN by any reasonable definition, because the number was input not predicted. We do NOT relabel the row "not a WIN" — we report provenance: fit and let the reader see the consequence.

  unclear — Cannot determine from published materials whether the value is forward or fit. Treated conservatively: row_notes records "provenance could not be determined from published materials; queried author for clarification [date]". Does not change the score.

== MATH-WRONG HANDLING ==

Five sub-cases, each with distinct scoreboard handling:

  CASE A — Published value does not reproduce from the formula the author claims derives it. Handling: dome_prediction_as_published_value quotes his number; dome_prediction_formula_check_value shows what our independent V51.1 implementation produces; formula_reproduces: false. The published value still drives win_by_own_standard. Row annotation: "author's published value does not reproduce from stated V51.1 derivation; independent implementation yields [X]."

  CASE B — Formula is fit, not forward. Handled by provenance: fit. See above.

  CASE C — Internal inconsistency: author publishes multiple dome values for the same quantity in different places (V51.1 paper, claim_index.json, review-response.html, model.html). Handling: list all values with sources in dome_prediction_as_published_source; win_by_own_standard: "disputed" until the author picks one. This is the strongest row-level critique available because the author cannot both claim the WIN and dispute which value we should score.

  CASE D — Published value matches observation, but only due to arithmetic error; correct derivation would NOT match. Handling: credit win_by_own_standard (because published value matches observation — take him at his word), but formula_reproduces: false with row annotation showing the corrected dome value and that it does not match observation. The reader sees: WIN credited against published value; independent math would have failed.

  CASE E — Published value does NOT match observation, but correct derivation WOULD match. Handling: no credit on win_by_own_standard (published value failed), but formula_reproduces: false with row annotation: "independent V51.1 calculation yields [Z], which matches observation. If the author corrects his published value to [Z], this row becomes a WIN-by-own-standard." This is a gift to the author; either response (accept correction / reject correction) is productive.

CRITICAL CONSTRAINT on formula checks: any time we compute dome_prediction_formula_check_value, we take on a correctness burden. If our V13/V51.1 implementation is wrong, we publish a false methodological flag. Mitigations (all required):
  - Implement every formula as reviewable code in a new directory: build-scripts/scoreboard-formulas/. One script per formula. Unit tests per script.
  - Each script cites the specific V51.1 section/equation it implements. Comments tag every non-trivial step.
  - Curmudgeon reviews every formula script adversarially before the corresponding row is published.
  - Rounding tolerance per row is stated explicitly in rounding_tolerance_applied. "Published 4,350 / formula 4,348" at 1% tolerance does NOT trip formula_reproduces: false; "Published 4,352 / formula 4,108" at 1% tolerance DOES.
  - For the 15-row first pass, include ONLY rows where the formula is simple enough to implement correctly with high confidence. Complex derivations (e.g., full eclipse ensemble modeling) stay off the first-pass scoreboard until we have a vetted implementation in a second pass.

== PHASED ROLLOUT ==

PHASE 1 — 15-row first pass. 9 rows classified in claim_index.json (win-069-australia-scaffold, win-068-eclipse-ensemble, win-058-bedrock-kappa, win-058-theta-rule, win-011-mohe-contact-phase, win-056-solar-elevation, win-062-tesla-wave-speed, pred-solar-009, pred-eclipse-tier3) + 6 kill-shot rows (Sydney-Perth, Polaris post-window, Aug 12 2026 eclipse EBR, NMP drift, SAA African cell, JFK-LHR aetheric slipstream). For each: dome_prediction_as_published sourced from author's materials where published, blank where not. Globe prediction sourced from named authority. Observation sourced authoritatively. win_by_own_standard and discriminating computed per Rule 2. Formula-check column populated only where V51.1 is simple enough for a reviewable implementation. Provenance field populated per taxonomy.

PHASE 1 OUTPUT. Scoreboard tab renders the 15 rows plus a "WINs with no published dome value" block listing the remaining (total WINs - rows-with-values) row IDs with blank cells and a shared footnote: "The dome author has not published specific numerical dome predictions for these rows. Under his own definition of a WIN, these cannot be confirmed predictions. We invite the author to populate dome predictions for any of these rows; we will update the Scoreboard and date-stamp the additions."

PHASE 1 HEADLINE. Something like: "Of [69] confirmed WINs, the dome author has published a specific numerical prediction for [9]. Of those [9], [X] match observation by the author's standard. Of those [X], [Y] are distinct from the globe's prediction. The remaining [69-9=60] WINs have no published dome prediction and cannot be WINs under the author's own definition." All bracketed numbers computed from data at build time per CLAUDE.md "never hardcode counts" rule.

PHASE 2 — Full 85-row scoreboard. After Phase 1 publication and author response (or silence over [N days]), expand the scoreboard to cover the remaining 60 WINs by: (a) exhaustive search of monitor.py / model.html / V51.1 PDF / audit.html / review-response.html / claim_index.json / methodology.json for any published dome value, (b) filling in where found, (c) blank where not found after exhaustive search with a note "searched [files]; no specific dome prediction found." Schema, rules, guardrails unchanged from Phase 1.

PHASE 2 CADENCE. Rate of filling depends on analyst/curmudgeon throughput. Estimate 15 minutes per row of analyst time + curmudgeon review + formula-script development where needed = ~20 hours of analyst time and similar curmudgeon time for a full 60-row expansion. Scope as a 4-6 week effort. Do NOT rush Phase 2 at the cost of correctness — a wrong globe number in even one cell impeaches the whole scoreboard.

== INTEGRATION WITH EXISTING REVIEW STRUCTURE ==

The Scoreboard is ADDITIVE, not replacement. Existing content stays:
  - Part 5 continues to per-kill-shot analysis (the scoreboard is row-by-row; Part 5 is the narrative). Scoreboard links INTO Part 5 per row.
  - Part 4 Live Power Dashboard continues.
  - Part 8b.2 sinuosity argument stands (scoreboard row for Sydney-Perth cites it).
  - Timestamp Error tab continues (scoreboard's pred-eclipse-tier3 row cross-links to it).
  - EXP-183 author_* fields on WINs continue (scoreboard's per-row display can surface author_claim_class next to the score cells).

Scoreboard becomes the LOAD-BEARING TAB for "how do the predictions actually score against observations?" questions. Overview tab updated to direct first-time readers to the Scoreboard before the narrative. Kill-Shot tab (currently rescoped to "strongest-evidence arguments") remains, but the Scoreboard becomes the authoritative per-row score.

== WHAT THIS DOES TO THE RETROSPECTIVE_STRUCTURAL LABEL ==

The author's own claim_index.json classifies 7 of 9 rows as retrospective_structural. That label is doing ambiguous work (see HNOTE on two-reading dilemma, Reading A "structural = globe structure" vs Reading B "structural = dome structure"). The scoreboard concretizes the ambiguity per row:
  - If a row's dome value (published) equals the globe value within uncertainty and matches observation, the row is *both / non-discriminating / WIN-by-own-standard*. This is Reading A made concrete: the structure doing the work is globe structure.
  - If a row's dome value (published) is distinct from globe and matches observation, the row is *dome-consistent, globe-inconsistent / discriminating / WIN-by-own-standard*. This is Reading B: a genuine dome win.
  - If provenance: fit, the row cannot be a WIN at all (see taxonomy).

The label "retrospective_structural" retires itself once the scoreboard is published. We don't argue the label — we let the scoreboard show which reading applies per row, and the label's ambiguity becomes unsustainable.

== ECLIPSE TIER-3 INTEGRATION (PRE-REGISTRATION SURFACE) ==

pred-eclipse-tier3 is the one entry in claim_index.json with strict_prospective_confirmed: true. Observation date: August 12 2026. It belongs in Phase 1 as a pending-observation row. Scoreboard schema fields:
  - dome_prediction_as_published_value: the tier-3 numerical threshold IF the author has published it. If not: BLANK with an annotation "tier-3 threshold not published as of [date]; author has stated a three-tier protocol exists but has not specified the numerical discriminating criterion."
  - globe_prediction_value: Chapman/standard-model forecast for the observable tier-3 targets, with uncertainty.
  - observation_is_pending: true.
  - observation_date: 2026-08-12.

Phase 1 Scoreboard publishes before August 2026. This gives the author a VISIBLE PLACE to publish the tier-3 threshold and associated OTS-timestamped prediction document. If the cell is still blank on August 11 2026, that is itself a finding: the strict_prospective_confirmed claim was not operationalised in time.

The Scoreboard thus becomes the pre-registration surface the OTS/canary critique has been asking for. Cross-link from the Timestamp Error tab to pred-eclipse-tier3's scoreboard row.

== COUNTER-CONSIDERATIONS THE ANALYST MUST ADDRESS IN THE EXPANSION ==

The EXP produced from this HNOTE MUST include explicit responses to each of the following anticipated objections. If the expansion does not address these, the curmudgeon review should reject it.

  OBJECTION 1 — "You're reducing cosmology to a scoreboard; theories are evaluated on explanatory unity, parsimony, theoretical virtues — not just row-by-row matches." ANALYST RESPONSE: the author framed the 69 WINs as 69 confirmed predictions. The scoreboard evaluates the object the author put on the table. Theory-level evaluation is a separate conversation; the scoreboard does not preclude it, it just audits the claimed-predictions layer honestly.

  OBJECTION 2 — "You demand numerical dome values for every claim, but models include qualitative predictions." ANALYST RESPONSE: the schema includes a "qualitative" marker. Where the author has made a qualitative prediction with no numerical target, the cell is "qualitative: [quote]" rather than blank. Scoring collapses to "phenomenon observed? yes/no" + "globe also predicted the phenomenon? yes/no." The author does not get to retrofit qualitative status onto claims he previously numericalized elsewhere; if any dome publication has a number, that number is the prediction.

  OBJECTION 3 — "Your globe numbers are wrong, therefore the whole scoreboard is impeached." ANALYST RESPONSE: globe numbers are sourced from named authoritative references (IGRF, Chapman, IAU, standard geodesy). Disputes are welcomed and handled per-row: "author disputes our IGRF-14 lookup; under-review." The scoreboard as a whole survives individual-row disputes because each row is independently sourced.

  OBJECTION 4 — "You're forcing me into a binary I didn't commit to — not every claim is win-or-lose." ANALYST RESPONSE: the scoreboard has three outcome columns, not one. win_by_own_standard records whether the dome value matches observation (the author's own criterion). discriminating records whether the dome differs from globe. row_notes carries narrative. We do not collapse to a binary; we expose the structure.

  OBJECTION 5 — "You didn't fill every dome cell either — your scoreboard is as incomplete as mine." ANALYST RESPONSE: we explicitly do NOT fill cells the author did not publish. That is a design invariant, stated publicly in the intro paragraph. The blanks are the author's to fill; we will update and date-stamp any additions.

  OBJECTION 6 — "You're using standard physics to derive dome predictions and then comparing them to observations; the comparison is rigged." ANALYST RESPONSE: Rules 1 and 2 and Guardrails G1-G5 explicitly prohibit this. Dome values we derive from globe physics never enter the score cells. They appear only in the formula-check annotation column, clearly labeled, and never drive win_by_own_standard or discriminating. The intro paragraph states this invariant.

  OBJECTION 7 (from our side — curmudgeon will raise it) — "The review is 90% blank cells; this looks like we're just listing the 60 unconfirmed rows to embarrass the author." ANALYST RESPONSE: the blanks ARE a finding. Each represents a WIN-claim without a published prediction. We do not embellish or moralize in the row itself; the blank and its footnote are the entire argument. The Scoreboard's honest report of a 9-filled, 60-blank ratio is accurate, not polemical.

== REQUIRED PIPELINE PATH ==

This HNOTE is a FOUNDATIONAL MODEL CHANGE. The required path ends at a HOLD POINT; there is no STEP that produces publication.

  STEP 1 (analyst, Mode 2). Read this HNOTE in full. Produce an EXP-NNN that proposes:
    (a) the scoreboard schema extensions to data/wins.json, data/predictions.json, and new data/killshots.json;
    (b) the 15-row Phase 1 content (actual values where sourcing is feasible within one analyst run; placeholders flagged for subsequent runs where deeper V51.1 work is needed);
    (c) the new top-level tab HTML structure and intro paragraph copy (verbatim, with the guardrail frame stated);
    (d) the build-scripts/scoreboard-formulas/ directory skeleton (at least two formula scripts with unit tests — V13 Finsler for Sydney-Perth and one other simple case — to demonstrate the pattern);
    (e) integration points with Part 5, Part 4, Timestamp Error tab, and EXP-183 author_* fields.
   
   At the top of the EXP JSON, set:
     status: "DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW"
     expansion_type: "FOUNDATIONAL_MODEL_CHANGE"
     integration_blocked_until: "operator posts HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-001"

  STEP 2 (decider, triage). Does NOT auto-integrate the EXP. Does NOT attempt to integrate the EXP on this run or any subsequent run until the named operator approval HNOTE is posted. Routes the EXP to curmudgeon with a REQUIRE_ADVERSARIAL_REVIEW flag and enqueues it. See companion HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-PIPELINE-ROUTING-001 in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json for the strict decider holdpoint rules.

  STEP 3 (curmudgeon, adversarial review). Treats the EXP as a hostile submission. Writes a review in monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ as usual. Review output is FOR HUMAN CONSUMPTION. It does NOT authorize decider integration by itself. Specifically attacks:
    - Does the schema enforce Rules 1-5? Are blanks actually unscored, or are they nulls that coerce to "no"?
    - Do the formula scripts in build-scripts/scoreboard-formulas/ correctly implement the V51.1 derivation? Unit tests pass?
    - Are guardrails G1-G5 operational, or merely stated in prose?
    - Is the 15-row first pass populated from the AUTHOR'S publications, or did the analyst derive any values?
    - Does the intro paragraph state the take-him-at-his-word frame?
    - Do the 7 anticipated objections receive substantive responses in the EXP?
    - Are provenance labels per-row defensible against the Reading A / Reading B / fit distinction?
    - Is the "qualitative" marker well-defined for non-numerical claims?
    - Are the Phase 1 vs Phase 2 boundaries clean and defensible?

  STEP 4 — HOLD POINT. At this point the pipeline STOPS. The draft EXP + curmudgeon review are ready artifacts for the operator's human group to bat around. Decider does NOT proceed. Analyst does NOT re-extend. Nobody integrates. Nobody publishes.

  STEP 5 (CONDITIONAL, requires operator action). If and only if the operator posts HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-001 in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json, with an approved_curmudgeon_review_id referencing the specific review from Step 3, decider may proceed with integration. That approval HNOTE does not currently exist and will only exist after human-group iteration completes, which may involve multiple rounds of analyst revisions and curmudgeon re-reviews before approval. Until the named approval HNOTE exists, the EXP stays in DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW status.

  STEP 6 (poller, deferred). Only after Step 5 integration: add to load-bearing pages list per Step 5b (dedicated chg file on any status-column or badge change, strategic classification).

The phrase "FOUNDATIONAL MODEL CHANGE" in this HNOTE's summary is the trigger for the required-adversarial-review routing AND the hard integration-hold. Analyst: include "FOUNDATIONAL_MODEL_CHANGE" in the expansion_type field so decider picks up both the routing and the hold.

== REQUIRED READING (ANALYST AND CURMUDGEON) ==

  - CLAUDE.md (this repo root)
  - data/sections.json — Part 4 (Live Power Dashboard), Part 5 (kill-shots), Part 8b (sinuosity, Chapman mechanism, Timestamp Error cross-links)
  - monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-183.json (author_* schema extension — parallel schema change, scoreboard adds to it)
  - monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-170.json (claim_index.json and methodology.json provenance)

  - Dome site: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/methodology.json (5-class taxonomy)
  - Dome site: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/ai_manifest.json (prospective_confirmed: 10)
  - Dome site: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/killshot.html (current state of the 6 kill-shot rows)
  - Dome site: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/review-response.html (anti-strawman framing)

== NOT COVERED BY THIS HNOTE ==

The following are OUT OF SCOPE for the Phase 1 expansion; analyst should flag as separate follow-ups in the EXP but not attempt to resolve in this work:

  - A CSS/design treatment for the Scoreboard tab beyond a functional table. Visual design is a second-pass concern.
  - Interactive filters/sort on the table. Static HTML is sufficient for Phase 1.
  - Machine-readable exports (JSON/CSV) of the scoreboard for third-party audit. Useful eventually; not Phase 1.
  - Historical version control of per-row edits (who changed what when). The git log suffices for Phase 1.
  - Integration with the dome's own API (if they publish updates to claim_index.json, we update our scoreboard). Future work.

END OF HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001.

notes.text:
================================================================================
HARD HOLD POINT — READ FIRST
================================================================================

This is a FOUNDATIONAL presentation-layer change to the review site. It is not
an ordinary patch.

Every EXP you produce in response to this brief MUST carry:
  - status: "DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW"
  - expansion_type: "FOUNDATIONAL_MODEL_CHANGE"  (treat as structural, same
    scoring level as HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001)
  - integration_blocked_until: "HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-
    APPROVED-NNN (operator-posted, author: operator, status: pending,
    references specific EXP ID)"
  - human_note_source: "HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001"
  - companion_note: "HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-ROUTING-001"

Integration unlock for any individual EXP requires ALL FOUR of:
  (a) an approval HNOTE exists in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json with
      id matching "HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN"
  (b) the approval HNOTE has field "approved_exp_ids": [...] containing
      this specific EXP's item_id
  (c) author: "operator", status: "pending" at the time the decider reads it
  (d) if the approval HNOTE references a curmudgeon review ID, that review
      must not be superseded

No other unlock mechanism exists. Pipeline-default behavior ("curmudgeon said
ok so integrate") is NOT sufficient. Decider MUST not self-approve.

If you are uncertain about any part of this brief, the safe action is to
produce a DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW EXP anyway and let the human
review group clarify. Do not skip items because they look ambiguous; surface
the ambiguity in notes_for_human_review_group instead.

================================================================================
CONTEXT
================================================================================

An external design review of our site was produced by Claude Design on 2026-
04-17 and provided to the operator as a handoff bundle. The review is
preserved verbatim at:

    monitor/analyst/design-review-2026-04-17-external.html

Please read that document before planning EXPs. It is self-contained (single
HTML file, no imports) and identifies nine findings plus three redesign
proposals. Its organizing thesis is:

    "The content is careful; the design asks the reader to take the author's
     word for it. A debunking document's strongest credibility signal is
     restraint."

The operator agrees with the thesis, and has accepted (with modifications) a
subset of the findings. This brief specifies that subset.

================================================================================
SCOPE — SEVEN ITEMS (PHASE A)
================================================================================

Item 1 — MASTHEAD STANCE STATEMENT  (new; not in the external review)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
A short preamble paragraph at masthead level that declares the document's
stance, so the rest of the design does not have to signal that stance
chromatically. Draft language (operator-approved, analyst may refine for
cadence but must preserve the four load-bearing moves):

    The world is a globe. The dome model fixes few or none of the
    fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology. That does not prevent us
    from engaging with the specific claims it does make — and that is what
    this document does.

Placement: above the hero thesis sentence in the masthead, at metadata/eyebrow
weight (NOT hero weight). The hero thesis sentence (to be agreed with the
human review group in a subsequent EXP, candidate below) remains the visual
hero.

Candidate hero thesis (analyst should treat as candidate, not final):
    None of the 69 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard
    physics cannot already explain.

Acceptance criterion: a reader flipping between the dome site and our site
should be able to tell, from the masthead alone, that the site they are
currently on is the review and that the review is operating from a
globe-earth frame.

Item 2 — VERDICT DISTRIBUTION BAR CHART ON OVERVIEW  (external review F3 / R1)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
The verdict distribution (Misleading 22, Std Model Explains 18, Self-
Contradicted 14, Refuted by Data 9, Not Demonstrated 4, Unfalsifiable 4 — as
of the current wins.json; counts computed live) is the single most compelling
data object we own. Present it as an ordered horizontal bar chart at the top
of the overview, with categories sorted by count. Each bar is a link to the
grouped section of the 69-WIN display.

Computed counts per CLAUDE.md rule: never hardcode. Read from wins.json at
build time. The existing build pipeline already computes these for the
scorecard cards; reuse that computation.

Acceptance criterion: on first load of the overview, the reader sees the
shape of the verdict distribution before any numeric stat grid.

Item 3 — ACCESSIBILITY AUDIT + CONCRETE FIXES  (external review F8)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
The external reviewer asserts verdicts are conveyed through color alone in
several places, some backgrounds drop below WCAG AA against their text, long
tables with sticky left columns become unusable at narrow widths, and tap
targets for internal anchors are small. Before producing a fix EXP, run a
proper audit using the design:accessibility-review skill against the live
docs/index.html to produce a specific defect list.

Required fixes from any audit:
  - Every color-coded verdict must also carry a glyph AND a label. Glyph +
    label travels with every verdict token, not just headers.
  - WCAG AA contrast ratio against the dark-theme background for all body
    and card text. Integrity has already flagged two hardcoded light-theme
    colors (#f5f5f5 on part1 WGS84 formula block, #fff on part6 Tesla patent
    card) — those must move to var(--card-bg) as part of this item.
  - Minimum 44px tap target for internal anchors and nav items.
  - Verdict tables collapse to stacked cards below 720px rather than
    reflowing into a narrow strip.

Acceptance criterion: automated WCAG AA contrast check passes, color-blind
simulation preserves verdict information, mobile verdict display is stacked-
card at sub-720px widths.

Item 4 — DEMOTE ACCURACY-MATH TO SIDEBAR  (external review F6)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
The "honest accuracy: 89.6%, not 94.5%" callout is important but has been
sitting at the same visual pitch as the thesis. It is a derived statistic
over a disputed denominator. Move it to a sidebar element at smaller visual
weight — retain the content and the arithmetic, reduce the display pitch.

Acceptance criterion: a skim-reader of the overview does not leave with the
accuracy percentage as the dominant remembered number. The thesis sentence
plus the verdict distribution outrank it.

Item 5 — DEMOTE "LATEST FINDINGS" WITHOUT BANISHING  (external review F7,
                                                     partial acceptance)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
The external reviewer recommended moving the Latest Findings ticker entirely
to the references page or footer. Operator explicitly rejects the "banish"
version and requires partial acceptance only. The Latest Findings block is
evidence that we are tracking a moving target, which is a credibility signal
— the dome site is actively updated and we demonstrably keep up. Demote
visual weight (smaller headlines, less chrome, lower on the overview) but do
not relocate away from the overview.

Acceptance criterion: the Latest Findings block is visibly subordinate to
the stance statement, the hero thesis, and the verdict distribution chart,
but is still discoverable on the overview without a tab switch.

Item 6 — THREE-TYPEFACE SYSTEM  (external review F4)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
Current state: body is system sans ('Segoe UI',Arial,Helvetica,sans-serif),
formulas are plain monospace, no serif anywhere, no webfonts loaded. The
document reads like a dashboard because one face does every job.

Adopt three typographic roles with clear assignments:
  --serif : argumentative prose — section bodies, WIN verdict text, TLDR
             text, finding descriptions, quotes, the masthead stance
             statement, and the hero thesis.
  --sans  : UI chrome and metadata — tab labels, nav, captions, pill
             labels, button text, section numbers, "kicker" eyebrows,
             status labels.
  --mono  : data evidence — all formulas, SHA hashes, URL fragments in
             prose, numeric values in tables, version strings.

Recommended stack (analyst may substitute with reasoned justification):
  serif : Source Serif 4
  sans  : Inter
  mono  : JetBrains Mono

Loading: via Google Fonts with preconnect, display=swap. All three are free,
widely cached, and the external reviewer's mock demonstrates they compose
well.

Dark-theme note: serif body text on a dark background generally wants
+50 weight vs. the same on paper. Analyst should prototype and eyeball
weight 400 vs 450 for body; headings can stay at 600.

Sections.json currently has 3 inline "font-family:monospace" occurrences in
prose; docs/index.html has more. All "font-family:monospace" literals get
replaced by a .formula class (or equivalent) that uses var(--mono).

Acceptance criterion: an element inspector can identify every body element
as using exactly one of the three roles. No orphan font-family declarations.
Integrity's build-reproducibility check passes unchanged.

Item 7 — RESTRICT RED TO ACTUAL IN-TABLE CONTRADICTIONS  (external review F2)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
Red is currently used as section chrome (Kill Shots tab header, "Wrong Side"
banners, large red numerals). The external reviewer argues this signals a
verdict before the reader has read a sentence, and that restrained chrome
lets the words do the verdict work.

Operator accepts the critique about visual weight but EXPLICITLY REJECTS any
proposal to rename "Kill Shots". The term is load-bearing content: it
signals that a specific subset of claims are severe enough to end the model
if true. Keep the term, lose the red chrome.

Required changes:
  - Section headers, nav chrome, and masthead banners use neutral ink, not
    red.
  - Oversized red numerals in stat blocks lose the red color; retain
    whatever weight/size they had.
  - Red survives only inside table cells that flag an actual contradiction
    (e.g., a "refuted" verdict pill inside a verdict table row). Pair with
    a glyph and a label per Item 3.
  - The Kill Shots tab LABEL remains "Kill Shots". The tab's visual styling
    becomes neutral (same weight as other tabs).

Acceptance criterion: running a page-wide color-usage audit, red accounts
for <5% of total colored pixels, and every red instance resolves to a
specific table-cell contradiction pair (verdict pill + refuted claim).

================================================================================
NON-NEGOTIABLE CONSTRAINTS
================================================================================

NC1 — DARK THEME STAYS. The external reviewer's mocks are paper/serif
editorial aesthetic. We are dark-theme and remaining dark-theme. Dark mode is
a deliberate visual differentiator — a reader flipping between the dome site
and our site reads the theme contrast as unconscious wayfinding ("I'm in the
review now, not the thing being reviewed"). Translate the external reviewer's
principles (restraint, typographic roles, restricted red) to dark-theme
equivalents; do NOT adopt a paper-theme CSS.

NC2 — TABS STAY. The external reviewer's F5 recommends killing tabs in favor
of a long-form scroll with a left rail. Operator explicitly rejects this.
Tabs give the reader a scope affordance — how big a document they are
entering. Tiered visual grouping within the existing tab bar is permitted
(and may be a Phase B item later), but the tab-based IA is preserved.

NC3 — "KILL SHOTS" TERM STAYS. See Item 7 above. Critique is about chrome
weight, not the term. Keep the term, neutralize the chrome.

NC4 — "LATEST FINDINGS" STAYS ON OVERVIEW. See Item 5. Demote; do not banish.

NC5 — COMPUTED COUNTS RULE. Every numeric claim in the new design surface
must be computed at build time from data/wins.json (or the appropriate data
file). Do not hardcode counts, percentages, or distributions anywhere in the
new design CSS, JS, or prose. This is the existing CLAUDE.md rule and
integrity will enforce it.

NC6 — BUILD REPRODUCIBILITY. After every Phase A EXP integrates, the
build-reproducibility check must still pass (fresh clone + node build.js
html produces identical docs/index.html up to the embedded date line). If a
change breaks reproducibility, the EXP must specify why and what integrity
should check instead.

NC7 — TESTS GREEN. test.js must pass at 3400+ (current baseline, will grow)
at every integration step. If an EXP adds new tests, it must state which
invariants they enforce.

================================================================================
DRAFT STANCE STATEMENT (operator-authored, for Item 1)
================================================================================

Verbatim text the analyst should use for the masthead stance statement:

    The world is a globe. The dome model fixes few or none of the
    fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology. That does not prevent us
    from engaging with the specific claims it does make — and that is what
    this document does.

Analyst may propose minor cadence revisions but the four load-bearing moves
must survive:
  (a) "The world is a globe." — declarative, first sentence, no hedging.
  (b) "fixes few or none of the fundamental problems of flat-earth
      cosmology" — partial concession; "few or none" leaves room for the
      model to have thought about some problems without conceding it has
      solved any.
  (c) "That does not prevent us from engaging" — the pivot; this is the
      reason the document exists at all.
  (d) "the specific claims it does make" — scope declaration; constrains
      what the rest of the document does to claim-level engagement.

Placement: masthead, above the hero thesis sentence, at eyebrow/metadata
weight (NOT hero weight). Suggested wrapper class: .stance-statement or
.masthead-preamble.

================================================================================
SUGGESTED EXP ORDERING (not required; analyst may resequence with reasoning)
================================================================================

The design tokens underpin everything else. Typography needs the tokens. The
stance statement, bar chart, and demotions all read differently once
typography lands. Palette restriction is best done after typography because
the red-vs-neutral decisions depend on typographic weight. A11y fixes touch
every surface and benefit from being last (once the other changes have
settled, the audit has a stable target).

Suggested sequence:
  EXP-A1  Design tokens (CSS custom properties): --serif, --sans, --mono,
          --ink, --ink-2, --rule, --accent, --warn, --crit, --good on dark.
          Load Google Fonts. No visual change yet; foundation only.
  EXP-A2  Typography application across docs/index.html and sections.json
          prose blocks. Replace font-family:monospace literals with .formula
          class. (Item 6)
  EXP-A3  Masthead stance statement. (Item 1)
  EXP-A4  Verdict distribution bar chart on overview. (Item 2)
  EXP-A5  Palette cleanup: restrict red to in-table contradictions; Kill
          Shots chrome neutralized but label preserved. (Item 7)
  EXP-A6  Accuracy-math sidebar demotion. (Item 4)
  EXP-A7  Latest Findings visual demotion on overview. (Item 5)
  EXP-A8  Accessibility audit + fixes (glyph+label, WCAG AA, 44px, mobile
          stacked cards). (Item 3)

Each EXP is independently operator-approvable. Approvals can batch (one
HNOTE approves several EXPs) or be per-EXP; operator will decide when the
drafts land.

================================================================================
REQUIRED EXP STRUCTURE
================================================================================

Each EXP JSON file (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-NNN.json) in response to
this brief must include at minimum:

  item_id                              (e.g., "DESIGN-REFRESH-TYPOGRAPHY")
  status                               = "DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW"
  expansion_type                       = "FOUNDATIONAL_MODEL_CHANGE"
  integration_blocked_until            = [per HARD HOLD POINT above]
  human_note_source                    = "HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001"
  companion_note                       = "HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-ROUTING-001"
  target                               (file paths this EXP touches)
  category                             = "design-refresh-phase-a"
  scope_item                           (which of Items 1–7 this EXP addresses)
  content_diff                         (exact CSS/HTML changes proposed)
  acceptance_criteria                  (how to verify the EXP succeeded)
  anticipated_objections               (at least three; see below)
  counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_address
                                       (see the seven objections below)
  non_negotiable_constraints_preserved (which NCs this EXP honors and how)
  kernel_of_truth                      (what the external reviewer got right
                                        for this item specifically)
  preserved_load_bearing_content       (e.g., "Kill Shots" term, Latest
                                        Findings block, dark theme)
  out_of_scope_for_this_exp            (what this EXP doesn't try to do)
  notes_for_human_review_group         (any ambiguity or tradeoff the group
                                        should weigh in on)

================================================================================
COUNTER-CONSIDERATIONS THE ANALYST MUST ADDRESS
================================================================================

The external reviewer was explicit about being "neutral on subject-matter
dispute, opinionated on design." That means some of their "chrome" vs.
"argument" classifications treat load-bearing content as decoration. Every
EXP must explicitly address how it preserves content that the external
reviewer may have misclassified:

CC1 — "Kill Shots" is not chrome. It is a content label. Any EXP touching
     the Kill Shots tab must explain how it neutralizes chrome weight while
     preserving the semantic force of the term.

CC2 — The 4+4 failure accounting is calibration for the honest-accuracy
     arithmetic. Demotion must preserve the ability to reach the
     arithmetic from the overview in one scroll.

CC3 — The Latest Findings block is evidence we track a moving target. This
     is content, not news-ticker chrome. Demotion must preserve
     discoverability.

CC4 — Dark theme is differentiator, not oversight. Any EXP proposing palette
     changes must start from dark-theme tokens, not from the external
     reviewer's paper palette.

CC5 — The hero thesis sentence candidate is a CANDIDATE. Any EXP that sets
     the hero thesis must flag it as up-for-human-review, not final.

CC6 — The verdict distribution chart derives from wins.json. Any EXP
     producing it must route through the existing build pipeline
     (generate-html.js) and respect the CLAUDE.md computed-counts rule.

CC7 — The stance statement is operator-authored. Any EXP touching the
     masthead must treat the four load-bearing moves in the draft text as
     non-negotiable, subject only to cadence refinement.

================================================================================
OUT OF SCOPE FOR PHASE A
================================================================================

The following items from the external review are DEFERRED and must NOT be
included in any Phase A EXP:

  - F1 overview hero rewrite to a single thesis sentence  → Phase C (pending
     human group decision on thesis text; the stance statement in Item 1
     provides part of the hero real estate for now).
  - F5 kill-tabs-in-favor-of-scroll  → REJECTED (see NC2); tiered visual
     grouping within tabs is a possible Phase B item but not part of Phase A.
  - R2 cluster-by-verdict restructure of the 69-WIN display  → Phase C;
     requires a significant rework of the wins tab and interacts with
     scoreboard schema (EXP-190).
  - F9 / R3 "What would change our mind" module  → SUBSUMED by EXP-190
     (the scoreboard). Do not build a separate R3 module; the scoreboard
     Phase 1 content is the surface this module was asking for.

================================================================================
CROSS-REFERENCES
================================================================================

Related holds and work:
  - HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001 (monitor/analyst/human-notes.json) —
    separate hard-hold EXP-190. Phase A design refresh must not integrate
    into the same files as scoreboard (data/wins.json schema, new
    killshots.json, build-scripts/scoreboard-formulas/) except where
    explicitly surfaces — e.g., if the bar chart and the scoreboard both
    want an overview block, the scoreboard block subsumes the bar chart.
  - External review source: monitor/analyst/design-review-2026-04-17-external.html
  - Integrity's current warn-level findings (broken URL, two hardcoded
    light colors) — Item 3 of this brief subsumes the two hardcoded-color
    fixes; the broken URL is out of scope here (decider already fixed it).

================================================================================
NOT COVERED
================================================================================

If the analyst identifies a need that is not explicitly scoped above, the
default action is NOT to silently extend scope. Instead:
  1. Note the gap in notes_for_human_review_group of the relevant EXP.
  2. If the gap is small, propose a minimum-viable extension with
     justification.
  3. If the gap is large, produce a standalone HNOTE request to the
     operator asking for scope clarification BEFORE producing the EXP.

EXP-A3 — masthead stance statement (Item 1): operator-authored four-move preamble inserted above 'Critical Review' h1, italic serif eyebrow weight, no hero thesis in this EXP.

notes.subject: EXP-202 clarification: self-host fonts, no third-party runtime deps
notes.body:
Operator decision on the Google Fonts question you flagged in EXP-202 anticipated_objections #2.

DECISION: Self-host the three WOFF2 font families. No third-party runtime dependencies.

RATIONALE
The dome review is a trust-building exercise. The current site has zero third-party network requests — that is a property worth preserving. A reader who opens DevTools Network and sees only same-origin requests is getting a small but real credibility signal: the critique has nothing to hide, no analytics, no CDN, no font company logging their IP. Adding Google Fonts trades that signal for convenience, and convenience is cheap here (WOFF2 subsets are small, licenses are SIL OFL, no font-loading pipeline complexity worth worrying about).

STANDING CONSTRAINT (amendment to HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 non-negotiables)
Add NC8: "No third-party runtime dependencies." All CSS, fonts, scripts, and images must be served from same-origin. Exception: hyperlinks to external references (dome author's site, published papers, GitHub) are expected and unaffected — this rule is about runtime network requests, not link targets.

WHAT TO DO FOR EXP-202
1. Revise EXP-202 in place (update the existing file; this is a PROP-009 additive-edit-friendly revision since status stays DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW).
2. Replace the Google Fonts <link> tags with self-hosted @font-face declarations in the <style> block.
3. Add the three WOFF2 files to the repo. Suggested path: docs/fonts/source-serif-4-variable.woff2, docs/fonts/inter-variable.woff2, docs/fonts/jetbrains-mono-variable.woff2 (variable-weight WOFF2 — single file per family covering all weights, ~40-90KB each). If variable isn't feasible for a given family, fall back to per-weight WOFF2 subsets (regular + bold + italic at minimum).
4. Use font-display: swap on each @font-face (same behavior Google Fonts served).
5. Font subsetting: latin subset only (U+0000-U+007F plus the basic latin-1 supplement range) is sufficient for this site. If you are unsure how to subset, note that in the revised EXP and propose a small build script under build-scripts/.
6. Update the file_ownership proposal in the revised EXP: docs/fonts/*.woff2 should be classified 'git' in OWNERSHIP (build.js) and OWNED_BY_GIT (workspace-sync.md), since they are static served assets. This is a new directory entry — flag it so decider knows to add it when integrating.
7. Update acceptance_criteria:
   - AC3 should change from "Network panel shows Google Fonts WOFF2 files downloaded" to "Network panel shows ZERO third-party requests. Three same-origin WOFF2 files download exactly once per page load."
   - Add a new AC: "curl -s http://localhost:8000/ -o /dev/null -w '%{num_redirects}' and a tcpdump / HAR capture both show no requests to fonts.googleapis.com, fonts.gstatic.com, or any non-origin host."
8. Keep NC1-NC7 responses; add NC8 to the non-negotiable_constraints_preserved list with the response "EXP-A1 self-hosts all three WOFF2 files under docs/fonts/. @font-face declarations are same-origin. No preconnect/dns-prefetch to third-party hosts anywhere in the <head>."

LOOK-AND-FEEL ESCAPE HATCH
Operator accepts self-hosting on the premise that rendering quality will be indistinguishable from CDN. If during EXP-A2 (the first consumer EXP) you find the self-hosted output degrades in some measurable way (hinting, subpixel rendering, missing glyphs in a range actually used on the site, FOUT that is worse than CDN FOUT), flag it back as a new human_note with:
  - Specific visual evidence (screenshots or described behavior)
  - Whether it is a subsetting bug, a variable-font limitation, or a genuine same-origin WOFF2 regression
  - Whether switching to self-hosted static WOFF2 (non-variable) resolves it
Do NOT silently revert to Google Fonts. The no-third-party rule is non-negotiable; if the look-and-feel bar cannot be met with same-origin assets, operator will revise the rule explicitly.

DO NOT
- Do not change the expansion_type (stays FOUNDATIONAL_MODEL_CHANGE).
- Do not change the integration_blocked_until value — the hold stays on EXP-202 until operator approves the REVISED version.
- Do not spawn an EXP-A1b for the font hosting change; revise EXP-202 in place. This is a clarification to the same EXP, not a new piece of work.
- Do not pull the font files from Google's CDN at build time and check them in. Pull them once manually (or use npx fontsource/@fontsource equivalent), verify they are SIL OFL, commit.

LICENSE NOTE
All three target families are SIL OFL: Source Serif 4 (Adobe, OFL), Inter (rsms, OFL), JetBrains Mono (JetBrains, OFL). Redistribution by self-hosting is explicitly permitted. Include a LICENSE or NOTICE file under docs/fonts/ referencing each license; the OFL does not require preserving filenames but does require the license notice to be distributed with the font.

CROSS-REFERENCES
- Original brief: HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001
- EXP being clarified: monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json
- External review source: monitor/analyst/design-review-2026-04-17-external.html
