NC8 'no NEW third-party runtime deps' introduced in non_negotiable_constraints_preserved (subsequently amended by CLARIFICATION-002 to grandfather GA). status/expansion_type/integration_blocked_until preserved per the explicit DO-NOT list in the clarification.

notes.subject: NC8 amendment: grandfather Google Analytics (one named exception); fix EXP-202 c_4
notes.body:
Correction to CLARIFICATION-001 and to your own EXP-202 anticipated_objections #2 response.

FACTUAL CORRECTION (the part you and I both got wrong)
docs/index.html is NOT zero-third-party-requests. It loads Google Analytics via googletagmanager.com (gtag.js, property G-QVD21HWH5X) in the <head> (see build-scripts/generate-html.js line ~1165). Curmudgeon flagged exactly this in monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/EXP-202-proposal.json, finding c_4 (severity: noted). The 'zero third-party network requests' premise in EXP-202's privacy objection response is false as stated, and that's a material misstatement of the site's current privacy posture. That finding is correct. Fix it.

OPERATOR DECISION ON GA
Grandfather Google Analytics (GA4 property G-QVD21HWH5X) as a single named exception to NC8. Reason: the site has effectively zero traffic right now and we need to see whether the discoverability push actually works. GA pageview data is a cheap way to observe the first organic arrival. This is explicitly a temporary carve-out; when meaningful organic traffic shows up in Search Console performance reports, we revisit whether to rip GA out and rely solely on Search Console signals.

REVISED NC8 (supersedes the CLARIFICATION-001 wording)
NC8: "No third-party runtime dependencies, with ONE grandfathered exception: the Google Analytics GA4 property currently configured at G-QVD21HWH5X (loaded via googletagmanager.com/gtag/js). No other third-party runtime dependencies are permitted. Hyperlinks to external references remain unaffected."

The exception is bounded three ways:
  1. Scope: the named property only. A new GA property, a second tag manager, a new analytics vendor      — all disallowed without a fresh HNOTE.
  2. Surface: only the existing gtag snippet in generate-html.js. Do not expand GA usage (no custom      events, no enhanced-measurement add-ons, no GA-served fonts or pixels) without a fresh HNOTE.
  3. Duration: 'until meaningful organic traffic arrives' — sunset trigger, not a date. Analyst or      social agent should flag when 20+ distinct-day organic Search Console clicks appear, at which      point operator reconsiders removal.

WHAT TO DO IN EXP-202 (revised version)
1. Fix the anticipated_objections #2 response. Replace any language claiming 'zero third-party    requests' with the honest framing: 'one grandfathered third-party runtime dependency (GA4) exists;    Phase A adds zero new ones. Self-hosted fonts preserve the privacy posture as it stands under the    revised NC8.'
2. Update the NC8-compliance acceptance criterion to match revised NC8: Network panel should show    'exactly one third-party request (gtag.js and its beacons to googletagmanager.com /    google-analytics.com) plus zero others' — not 'zero third-party requests.'
3. Add an assertion to the acceptance criteria: 'No new third-party hosts appear in the Network panel    beyond the existing GA endpoints (googletagmanager.com, google-analytics.com, region.analytics.google.com).'
4. In the non_negotiable_constraints_preserved block, update the NC8 response to quote the revised    rule verbatim and note that GA is preserved (not added) by this EXP.
5. When revising, this is still an additive/in-place edit of DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW. Do    not change status, expansion_type, or integration_blocked_until.

WHAT TO DO RE CURMUDGEON c_4
c_4 on EXP-202-proposal is a legitimate hit. When you revise EXP-202, add an entry to the EXP's curmudgeon_finding_responses (or equivalent field) that explicitly acknowledges c_4, describes the correction (honest GA framing + NC8 amendment), and references HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-CLARIFICATION-002 by ID. The decider pipeline can then close c_4 as addressed on integration.

DO NOT
- Do not remove the GA tag from generate-html.js. The exception is explicit: GA stays, for now.
- Do not try to 'compensate' for GA by adding a same-origin logger or server-side analytics. If we   want additional measurement, that is a separate HNOTE.
- Do not re-open the self-hosted-fonts decision. CLARIFICATION-001 stands on that.

CROSS-REFERENCES
- Root brief: HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001
- Fonts clarification (amended by this note): HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-CLARIFICATION-001
- Curmudgeon finding being resolved: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/EXP-202-proposal.json c_4
- GA tag location: build-scripts/generate-html.js line ~1165
- Rendered GA tag: docs/index.html lines 7-13

GA tag in build-scripts/generate-html.js is untouched per the explicit DO-NOT instruction.

notes.subject: Phase B design refresh — commission 3 EXPs (pie chart cleanup, DOCX typography, prefix unification). Each standalone; write as separate EXP files.
notes.body:
CONTEXT

Phase A1 landed (EXP-202/207/208/209/210/212/213 integrated). EXP-211-redesign is the last Phase A loose end and is revision-pending under ISS-1425. Separately, several items were explicitly deferred out of Phase A scope with the label 'Phase B' scattered across EXP-207/209/210/211-redesign notes. Operator gathered them; this HNOTE formally commissions three Phase B EXPs.

WRITE AS THREE SEPARATE EXP FILES (use the next three tracker next_id slots — as of this writing next_id=245). Each chunk is standalone; no cross-file dependencies. Analyst may deliver in any order.

=== PHASE B1 — PIE CHART CLEANUP ===

Scope: the verdict pie chart in WINS tab Part 3.1 (rendered via generateVerdictPie() or similar in build-scripts/generate-html.js). Four jobs:
1. Tokenize pie-slice colors. Slice fills for stdmodel / refuted / self-contradicted / misleading currently hardcoded or only partially tokenized. Route them through the same CSS variables that drive td.v-* verdict encoding elsewhere on the page (per EXP-210 color-token work). The red IS a verdict-encoded data color — it earns its red the same way td.v-refuted does.
2. Fix slice-label contrast. Existing labels on dark slice fills fail WCAG AA contrast. Either (a) move labels outside the slice with a leader line, or (b) enforce a light-text on dark-slice / dark-text on light-slice rule driven by the token set.
3. Enlarge legend-swatch tap targets. Current legend swatches are too small on mobile (<24px). Meet 44×44 CSS-pixel minimum per WCAG 2.5.5 (AAA) — since the site already claims WCAG 2.1 AA, meeting the AAA tap-target criterion is operator-preferred where feasible.
4. Re-render SVG with var() fills. Current SVG uses hex fills directly; change to var(--token-name) so theming flows through.

Boundary: PIE CHART ONLY. Do not touch other charts (verdict bar chart is EXP-209 territory, table verdict cells are EXP-210 territory). Single self-contained area of the HTML generator.

Out of scope for B1: any content or label-text changes, any redesign of the pie chart (doughnut vs pie, ordering, etc.).

Source refs: EXP-209 notes_for_human_review_group ('the chart's red IS a verdict-encoded data color... Phase B'), EXP-210 notes ('pie-chart SVG intentionally left untouched — flagged as Phase B'), EXP-211-redesign c* reviews if pie-chart-adjacent.

=== PHASE B2 — DOCX TYPOGRAPHY ALIGNMENT ===

Scope: update build-doc-v4.js to use the same font stack as the HTML build (Source Serif 4 serif body, Inter sans UI, JetBrains Mono code — per EXP-207).

Jobs:
1. Audit build-doc-v4.js current font declarations (likely uses Word's default Calibri / Times New Roman).
2. Replace with Source Serif 4 (body), Inter (any UI/caption text if applicable), JetBrains Mono (code spans).
3. Embed the WOFF fonts in the DOCX if the DOCX generator supports font embedding; otherwise rely on Word's font-substitution and document the substitution behavior in the EXP.
4. Render a test DOCX and spot-check visually against the HTML for typographic parity.

Boundary: DOCX PIPELINE ONLY. build-doc-v4.js is a separate generator; do not refactor it to share code with generate-html.js in this EXP. Cross-pipeline unification is a bigger refactor and not Phase B scope.

Out of scope for B2: DOCX content or layout changes; PDF export.

Source refs: EXP-207 notes ('DOCX export pipeline changes — build-doc-v4.js is a separate DOCX generator and is NOT touched by this EXP. If the group wants DOCX to adopt serif body, that is a Phase B or C follow-on').

Note: the woff2 files landed at 3770d41 live under docs/fonts/. If Word/DOCX won't consume woff2 (likely), analyst should source OTF/TTF variants from the same upstream (adobe-fonts/source-serif 4.005R, rsms/inter v4.0, JetBrains/JetBrainsMono v2.304) and commit the OTF/TTF files to docs/fonts/ alongside the woff2 set. License-compatible (all OFL-1.1).

=== PHASE B3 — DESIGN SYSTEM PREFIX UNIFICATION ===

Scope: consolidate the Phase A1 component class prefixes onto a single design-system prefix.

Current state (from EXP-209 notes): verdict bar uses .vb-, verdict legend uses .vl-, scorecard uses .sc-. Other components (Latest Findings, accessibility patches from EXP-213, etc.) may have additional ad-hoc prefixes. Analyst should inventory FIRST before refactoring.

Jobs:
1. Inventory every Phase A1 component class (grep generate-html.js for class='...' patterns, catalog unique prefixes).
2. Pick a single design-system prefix. Operator recommends .ds- (design-system, short, unambiguous, already in common industry use). Alternatives worth analyst consideration: .c- (component), .dmr- (dome-model-review). Analyst decides and justifies.
3. Rename every component class in generate-html.js and any sections.json references, output files, docs/index.html references, tests.
4. Verify no visual regression via build-then-diff against main.

Boundary: DO NOT rename .ps-* (progressive disclosure prose) or .ks-* (kill-shots). Those are structural scaffold classes with their own rationale per CLAUDE.md. They stay.

Out of scope for B3: any component redesign, any new components, any CSS reorganization beyond the rename.

Recommended sequencing: land B3 LAST in Phase B. If B3 ships before B1/B2, pie chart cleanup and DOCX work will introduce more classes that immediately need renaming, wasting a follow-up pass. If B1 and B2 ship first, B3 has a stable final class set to rename once.

Source refs: EXP-209 notes_for_human_review_group ('If the human group wants a single design-system prefix (e.g., ds- or c- for components), that is a Phase B / design-system pass and not a Phase A blocker').

=== EXECUTION NOTES ===

- Each EXP is independent; no decider-side sequencing required. Decider will assign them to the next three slots (expected EXP-245/246/247 barring parallel allocations).
- All three stay within Phase B scope — if analyst finds scope creep during execution (e.g., tokenization in B1 reveals an unrelated color bug elsewhere), file a new issue and DO NOT expand the EXP. This keeps review surface predictable.
- Curmudgeon review rubric for these is the same updated design rubric used for EXP-211-redesign (per DIRECTIVE-003). Analyst should read the current curmudgeon prompt's design section before writing so review criteria are baked into the EXP.
- No operator approval gate like Phase A1's human-group review. These are post-A1 polish; decider may integrate directly after curmudgeon clears.

=== SEPARATELY — HERO THESIS SENTENCE (NOT PART OF THIS COMMISSION) ===

EXP-208 deferred the masthead hero thesis sentence ('None of the 69 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain') out of Phase A. This was labeled 'Phase B/C' but is genuinely a Phase A tail — one sentence insertion in the masthead. NOT commissioned in this HNOTE. Operator will decide separately whether to commission it as a small Phase A follow-up patch or bundle into a later design pass.

=== DELIVERABLE ===

Three EXP files in monitor/analyst/expansions/, each with id field, target, priority, status='complete', integrated=false (decider will flip on integration), issue_ids (if any new ones are created during analysis), created_at, output_file path, and the standard expansion schema fields (replacement_html or similar per DATA-SCHEMAS.md).

Assume operator approval implicit for all three within the scope described above. Flag any scope questions back via a new analyst issue-proposal; do not block on waiting.

STATUS: open.

Out of scope per boundary: bar chart, table cells, redesign.

HNOTE was authored 2026-04-24, 17 days later, evidently from stale context. Current PDF generation uses Playwright headless Chromium loading docs/index.html via file://; the @font-face block at generate-html.js:495 (Source Serif 4 / Inter / JetBrains Mono variable WOFF2 from docs/fonts/) propagates to the PDF automatically. Typographic parity HTML↔PDF is structurally guaranteed; no DOCX-side font configuration to align. EXP-250 documents the finding in full with evidence. No code change. Recommend: close §B2 obsolete in this HNOTE; if DOCX revival is desired, file a fresh HNOTE with explicit scope (deps to restore, sections.json consumer to write, bookmark-ID strategy, build target wiring). Phase B sequencing unaffected — B1 already shipped (EXP-249), B3 (prefix unification) is the recommended next Mode 2 target.

A subsequent scheduled run (2026-04-25T17:12 — current run) read a stale tracker snapshot from the clean clone (items=248, next_id=250) before workspace-sync caught up, and produced a duplicate B3 EXP at slot EXP-250 (output_file monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-250-phase-b3-prefix-unification.json). notes.author: operator (steve, via cowork session)
notes.subject: Phase B addition — verdict-chip → definition click-through (F3 recommendation, missed from original Phase A commission)
notes.body:
CONTEXT

Cross-check of the Phase B commission (HNOTE-PHASE-B-DESIGN-REFRESH-COMMISSION-001) against the original Claude Design external review (monitor/analyst/design-review-2026-04-17-external.html) revealed one item that was silently dropped in the Phase A brief translation and never landed in Phase A1 or Phase B-v1.

The external review's F3 (Evidence UX) recommendation includes: "Every WIN then links from its verdict chip back to the definition." This is distinct from R2's full cluster-by-verdict regroup (deferred to Phase C). The chip→definition link is a light-lift click-through that reinforces the verdict taxonomy as the review's primary artifact (per Principle #3 in the external review §05).

Current state: the site shows verdict pills on each WIN row but there is no click-through to the verdict category's definition. grep of build-scripts/generate-html.js and docs/index.html for 'verdict definition' returns zero hits.

COMMISSION

Add this as a FOURTH Phase B EXP (commission companion to HNOTE-PHASE-B-DESIGN-REFRESH-COMMISSION-001). Expected EXP ID: next free slot after the three commissioned in note 001 (EXP-248 if note 001 takes 245/246/247).

SCOPE

1. Inventory: identify every place a verdict chip/pill appears in the rendered HTML. Candidates: 69-WIN table rows, Overview scorecard cells, Bar chart row labels, Kill Shots badges, anywhere else a verdict category is named as a styled element.

2. Define anchor targets: pick the canonical definition surface for each of the six verdict categories (Refuted by Data, Self-Contradicted, Misleading, Std Model Explains, Not Demonstrated, Unfalsifiable). Likely location: the Evaluation Guide tab already contains definitions; if so, add stable id attributes (e.g., id="def-refuted", id="def-self-contradicted"). If definitions are not currently on a single surface, add a small "Verdict definitions" block to the Overview or Evaluation Guide — 6 short paragraphs, one per category.

3. Wrap every verdict chip in an anchor link pointing to its category's definition id. Preserve existing chip styling; the anchor is a functional wrap, not a visual change. Use fragment identifiers (e.g., href="#def-refuted").

4. Accessibility: ensure the anchor has descriptive text (aria-label like 'See definition of Refuted by Data' if the visible text is abbreviated or icon-only).

5. Focus / hover affordance: chip gets a subtle underline or focus ring indicating it's now clickable. Operator prefers minimal chrome — a thin underline on hover, full focus ring on :focus-visible.

BOUNDARY

This is a FUNCTIONAL AUGMENTATION of existing pills, not a redesign. Do not change pill colors, sizes, or shapes. Do not regroup WINs by verdict (that's R2 / Phase C). Do not redesign the Evaluation Guide tab (scope may allow adding anchor ids and light formatting, but not reworking the content).

OUT OF SCOPE

- Cluster-by-verdict restructure of the 69-WIN table (Phase C)
- Any Evaluation Guide content changes beyond anchor id additions
- Tooltip on hover as an alternative to click-through (decided click-through is the cleaner pattern per F3 recommendation; hover is fragile on mobile)

SOURCE REFS

- External review F3, §03 Findings: 'Every WIN then links from its verdict chip back to the definition. See §R2.'
- External review Principle #3: 'Treat the taxonomy as the primary artifact. The six verdict categories are the review's most transportable idea. They should be legible from any point in the document and reinforced wherever an individual WIN is referenced.'
- My own cross-check 2026-04-24: gap identified when reconciling Phase B commission against the external review's 9 findings.

EXECUTION

Same pattern as HNOTE 001 — no operator approval gate (post-A1 polish), decider may integrate after curmudgeon clears, use updated curmudgeon design rubric from DIRECTIVE-003. If analyst finds the Evaluation Guide doesn't currently host verdict definitions cleanly, flag via notes_for_human_review_group rather than expanding scope unilaterally.

PRIORITIZATION

Low priority relative to the three commissioned in note 001. Can land before or after any of those; no sequencing dependency. If analyst is capacity-constrained, this is the first Phase B item to defer.

notes.author: decider (routing operator disposition)
notes.subject: Analyst task: EXP-211-redesign-v2 — address three curmudgeon findings before Phase A2 integration
notes.body:
CONTEXT: Operator sided with curmudgeon on ISS-1425 per HNOTE-ISS-1425-OPERATOR-SIDES-WITH-CURMUDGEON-001. EXP-211-redesign has three issues that must be resolved before Phase A2 integration.

FINDING #1 (ALREADY RESOLVED): Dropped "The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator." sentence — re-inserted per HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-REINSERT-HEADLINE-SENTENCE-001 (commit 3e2d24b). Verified present. No action needed.

FINDING #2 (FIX REQUIRED): <aside> semantic element creates landmark-skip a11y regression. Screen-reader users navigating via landmark shortcuts can skip the entire accuracy-math critique including the load-bearing denominator sentence. Resolution options per operator HNOTE: (a) use <div> with heading structure instead of <aside>; (b) keep <aside> but add role="region" with aria-label and visible h3 so content is navigable not skippable; (c) keep accuracy math inline and skip sidebar demotion. Do NOT ship with bare <aside>.

FINDING #3 (FIX REQUIRED): One-scroll → two-scroll reachability regression. Demoting accuracy math to sidebar pushes hardcoded-number critique below fold on common viewports. Resolution: revised EXP-211 must preserve one-scroll reachability on ~1080p/1440p desktop and typical mobile viewports. Include a concrete viewport check in acceptance_criteria (state the viewport tested).

ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENT: Add --ink-3 contrast acceptance criterion to acceptance_criteria list. Include WCAG 2.1 AA contrast check for demoted accuracy-math text color against its background (≥4.5:1 body text, ≥3:1 large text). State measured ratio in acceptance_criteria.

OUTPUT: Write revision as a NEW file (e.g., EXP-211-redesign-v2.json or similar append-only-safe name). Do NOT overwrite EXP-211.json or EXP-211-redesign.json. Update expansion-tracker.json to reference the new revision output file. Once written, decider will re-evaluate under the conditional approval rubric (HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-CONDITIONAL-001). No further operator HNOTE needed if curmudgeon re-review is clean.

PRIORITY: Normal. This is Mode 2 work for analyst — pick up after Mode 4 queue quiets per HNOTE-OPERATOR-PHASE-B-DRAIN-PRIORITY-003.

REF: ISS-1425 (closed as wontfix pending revision), HNOTE-ISS-1425-OPERATOR-SIDES-WITH-CURMUDGEON-001, HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-CONDITIONAL-001, HNOTE-OPERATOR-PHASE-B-DRAIN-PRIORITY-003.
